js/locales/en/ui_en.json — English UI strings. All user-facing strings from the app shell. Keys follow the convention: {file_or_feature}_{description}. Strings with {placeholders} use t(key, { var: value }) interpolation.
appinit_restore_study_error: Couldn't restore your last study. Opening the library instead.
main_no_study_body: It looks like you haven't selected a Bible study yet. Please visit your library to get started.
modals_likert_rotate_tip: 💡 Tip: turn your device sideways for a better view of the scale.
modals_notes_info_body:
Use this space for anything that doesn't fit neatly into the structured questions:

• Personal reflections on the chapter

• Jottings during a group discussion

• Prayer notes prompted by what you've read

• Questions to bring to a leader or mentor

These notes are included when you use the Share button for this chapter.
onboarding_app_slide1_body:
Several Bible studies are already loaded and ready for you.
  • Each one is a self-contained course with Bible passages, questions, and personal reflection built in.
  • Read, reflect, and write your answers as you go.
  • Your answers are saved automatically as you type.
  • You answers can receive feedback automatically (the default), or only when you press a button. You can change this behaviour in settings.
onboarding_app_slide2_body:
When the onboarding closes, you will see the Library — tap any study to open it.
  • More studies are available to download from the Load tab in the Library.
  • Studies come as .estudy files — you can also load one from your device if you have received one.
onboarding_app_slide3_body:
The How to Use page explains everything.
  • Tap the ? icon in the top navigation bar at any time.
Now let's take you to the Library so you can choose a study and get started!
onboarding_starring_slide1_body: Tap the star icon on any question card to mark it. Starred questions get a gold highlight so they stand out as you work through the chapter.
onboarding_starring_slide2_body: All your starred questions appear in a summary panel at the top of the chapter — and in the Progress Overview. Useful for group discussion prep or personal review.
onboarding_search_slide1_body: Tap the search icon in the top bar to search across all questions in the current study — and across everything you have written in your answer fields.
onboarding_search_slide2_body: Each result shows the question and your answer together. Tap any result to jump directly to that chapter and question.
onboarding_sharing_slide1_body: Tap Share at the bottom of any chapter to send that chapter's answers via WhatsApp, email, or any other app on your phone.
onboarding_sharing_slide2_body: To share all your answers in one go, go to Settings → Study → Data → Share all answers. There is also a Print / export all chapters option for saving as PDF.
onboarding_sharing_slide3_body:
Go to Settings → App → Saving & Sharing → Share format.

WhatsApp Formatted adds bold markers that WhatsApp renders as bold. Plain Text is cleaner for email. You can change it at any time.
onboarding_myprogress_slide1_body: Tap the progress icon in the top bar to open the Progress Overview — a summary of how many questions you have answered in each chapter, with a completion ring for the whole study.
onboarding_myprogress_slide2_body: All your starred questions from every chapter are collected in the Progress Overview — so you can review what mattered most across the whole study.
onboarding_voiceinput_slide1_body: A microphone icon appears inside answer fields on supported devices. Tap it to dictate your answer by voice — useful when typing on a small screen feels slow.
onboarding_voiceinput_slide2_body: Speak naturally and pause before tapping stop. The transcribed text appears in the answer field and can be edited freely afterwards. Works in any language your device supports.
onboarding_readaloud_slide1_body: A speaker icon on Bible passages and pop-ups lets the app read them aloud — useful for accessibility, for listening while commuting, or simply for a different way of engaging with the text.
onboarding_readaloud_slide2_body:
Go to Settings → App → Read Aloud to choose: Never, Long passages only, or Always.

This setting requires Advanced interface mode.
onboarding_biblepopups_slide1_body:
Wherever you see a small info icon beside a Bible reference, tap it to read the passage in a pop-up — without leaving the question you are working on.

All passages are from the NET Bible. Other info icons give background context or definitions.
onboarding_notesarea_slide1_body: Every chapter has a free-writing area at the very bottom — for anything that doesn't fit the structured questions: personal thoughts, things to look up, questions to bring to your group.
onboarding_notesarea_slide2_heading: Notes Are Included When You Share
onboarding_notesarea_slide2_body:
Your notes are included in the Share output and exported PDF — so nothing gets left behind.

Tip: long-press any answer field (including Notes) to copy its contents to the clipboard.
onboarding_autosave_slide1_body:
The app auto-saves your answer whenever you move away from a field — so you don't need to remember to save.

The Save button at the bottom of each page gives you an extra manual save whenever you want one.
onboarding_interfacemode_slide1_body:
The app has three modes: Basic, Intermediate, and Advanced. Basic gives you a clean experience with the essentials. The other modes unlock more features progressively.

You can switch at any time — no data is ever lost.
onboarding_interfacemode_slide2_body: Remember my place · Reading font · Answer box size · My Progress page · Notes & Comments page · Leaders' Notes · Library settings · App Updates
onboarding_interfacemode_slide3_body:
Icon style · Next/Previous buttons · Auto-save notification · Share format · Read Aloud controls · How to Use and About page toggles

Find Interface Mode at the bottom of Settings → App.
onboarding_library_slide1_body: Tap the Library icon (top-left) to see all your loaded studies. Tap any study to switch to it. Your answers in each study are kept completely separately.
onboarding_library_slide2_body: Tap "Load Study" at the bottom of the Library screen to import a .estudy file. Once loaded, the study is added to your list permanently and can be opened at any time.
onboarding_library_slide3_body:
Tap the 🗑️ icon on a study card to remove it.

This deletes the study and all your saved answers for it — permanently. Export or share your answers first if you want to keep them.
onboarding_progressbar_slide1_body:
The thin coloured line at the bottom of the top bar shows what proportion of questions in the current chapter you have answered. It updates as you save.

You can turn it on or off in Settings → App → Appearance → Show progress bar.
onboarding_swipenav_slide1_body: Swipe left to go to the next chapter, right for the previous one. You can also use the Next / Previous buttons at the bottom of each chapter (these can be shown or hidden in Settings).
onboarding_swipenav_slide2_body: If swiping feels too easy to trigger by accident — or too hard to register — go to Settings → App → Navigation → Swipe Sensitivity and choose Low, Medium, or High.
onboarding_rememberpos_slide1_body:
Turn on "Remember my place" in Settings → App → Appearance to return to your last chapter automatically when you reopen the app.

It also adds a Continue → button on the title page so you can pick up exactly where you left off.

Requires Intermediate interface mode or above.
onboarding_leadersnotes_slide1_body: Leaders' Notes provides pastoral guidance for group leaders and mentors — key teaching points and notes on sensitivities for each chapter.
onboarding_leadersnotes_slide2_body:
Go to Settings → App → Optional Pages → Leaders' Notes and toggle it on.

Requires Intermediate interface mode. Once enabled, Leaders' Notes appears in the Contents menu.
onboarding_answercheck_slide1_body:
A small check icon appears at the bottom-right of eligible question cards. Tap it to run a quick offline check of your answer — it looks at length, relevance, and whether your answer engages with the passage.

No internet connection is needed. Nothing is sent anywhere.
onboarding_answercheck_slide2_body: In Manual mode, the check only runs when you tap the icon yourself — so you stay in control of when you receive feedback. Tap the icon any time after writing your answer.
onboarding_answercheck_slide3_body: In Auto mode (the default), the check runs automatically each time your answer is saved — but only if you have changed your answer since the last check, so it never fires twice for the same text. Switch between Manual and Auto in Settings → App → Answer Check.
renderchapter_answer_tools_mic_desc: tap to dictate your answer by voice instead of typing. Only appears if your device supports speech input.
renderchapter_answer_tools_localvalidate: runs a quick offline check of your answer and gives brief feedback. In Manual mode you tap this icon yourself; in Auto mode it runs automatically after each save. You can change this in Settings → App.
renderchapter_answer_tools_aichat_desc: an optional AI tutor that gives fuller feedback on your answer. Off by default; turn it on in Settings → App.
renderchapter_eyebrow_info_title: Some features to look out for
renderchapter_eyebrow_info_body:
Tap the ☆ icon on a question card to star it. Starred questions are highlighted in gold and collected in a summary panel at the top of the chapter, so you can quickly find the questions you most want to return to or discuss. They also appear in your Progress Overview.

Tap the microphone icon inside any answer field to dictate your answer by voice instead of typing. The icon only appears if your device supports speech input.

The thin coloured line at the bottom of the top bar is your chapter progress bar — it fills as you answer questions in this chapter.

Your answers are saved automatically when you move away from an answer field — you don't need to do anything. The Save button is there if you want to save manually, for example before sharing your answers.
renderlib_all_empty_body: Go to the Load tab and load an .estudy file to get started.
renderlib_recent_empty_body: Studies you open will appear here for quick access.
renderlib_shelves_empty_body: Install studies to see them arranged by category.
renderlib_shelves_intro: Your installed studies are automatically sorted onto shelves by topic — Old and New Testament, Beliefs, Life, Ministry, and more. Use the shelves to browse what you have and jump straight to a study.
renderlib_shelves_popup_body:
When you install a Bible study, it declares which shelf it belongs to — for example "Old Testament", "Beliefs", or "Life". The Shelves view uses these tags to organise everything you have installed into one tidy place.

You can browse by shelf to see what you have in a particular area, or use it to spot any gaps in your reading — perhaps you have lots of New Testament studies but fewer from the Old Testament, or plenty of Belief studies but not much on Christian living.

Tap any study title to open it directly. If a shelf is showing sections (such as "Law" or "Gospels & Acts"), use Expand all to reveal the individual study rows inside each section.

By default, empty shelves are not shown – those with no studies actually installed – are not shown. If you want to see them, you can switch them on in the Settings/Library tab.
renderlib_paths_intro: Study pathways are groups of Bible studies that help you connect your learning together, and that allow you to better track your progress.
renderlib_paths_popup_body:
Learning pathways are curated collections of Bible studies designed to give you a rounded and structured learning experience. Rather than studying individual topics in isolation, a pathway guides you through a joined-up journey — helping you build on what you've learned as you go.

Each pathway aims to include studies drawn from across the breadth of Christian learning: the Old Testament, the New Testament, core Christian beliefs (theology), life (Christian living and discipleship), and ministry (serving and leading others). This means that whichever pathway you follow, you'll be engaging with a wide range of Scripture and topics — not just one area.

To make it easy to get started, we've bundled all the studies in each pathway together into a single zip file. Just tap the download icon next to a pathway to download and install everything at once.
renderlib_paths_hidden_note: {count} more section{count, plural, one{} other{s}} in this pathway are not shown here. To see them all, go to Settings → Library and increase the Paths setting.
renderlib_study_not_available: This study is not yet available, but we're working on it for a future release.
renderlib_pathway_not_available: This pathway is not yet available, but we're working on it for a future release.
renderlib_try_this_heading:
Not sure where to start?
Start here!
renderpages_newhere_hasstudy_body: If you’d like a quick overview of the study before diving in, tap the button below. It will show you the introductory slides for the {studyTitle} study again.
renderpages_newhere_nostudy_body: No study is loaded yet. Head to the Library to load a study — the app will walk you through an introduction the first time you open it.
renderpages_newhere_appslides_prompt: If you want to see the app introduction slides again, tap this button:
renderpages_topbar_library_desc: Tap the icon on the far left to open your Library — the list of study files you have loaded. From there you can switch studies or load a new one.
renderpages_topbar_search_desc: Tap the search icon to search across all questions and your saved answers in the current study.
renderpages_topbar_progress_desc: Tap the progress icon to open the Progress Overview — a summary of how many questions you have answered in each chapter, with a completion ring for the whole study.
renderpages_topbar_howto_desc: Tap the How to Use icon to open this page. It explains how to get the most from the study and walks through the app’s features.
renderpages_topbar_settings_desc: Tap the settings icon to open Settings. Key options: Text Size, Dark Mode, swipe sensitivity, answer box height, and share format.
renderpages_topbar_contents_desc: Tap the contents button to open the full chapter list. Tap any chapter to jump straight to it. Chapters with saved answers are marked with a ✓.
renderpages_topbar_progressbar_desc: The thin coloured line at the very bottom of the top bar shows how many questions in the current chapter have been answered. You can turn it on or off in Settings. (It doesn’t appear on ‘non-chapter’ pages like this page.)
renderpages_topbar_gotosettings_body: You can adjust the app’s appearance and behaviour — font size, dark mode, swipe sensitivity, and more — from the Settings page.
renderpages_chapters_biblepopup_desc: Where you see a small info icon beside a reference, tap it to read the passage in a pop-up. All passages are from the NET Bible. (Other info pop-ups will give you other information as well.)
renderpages_chapters_starring_desc: Tap the star icon on any question card to star it. Starred questions get a gold highlight and appear in a summary panel at the top of the chapter and in the Progress Overview.
renderpages_chapters_notes_desc: A free-writing area at the bottom of each chapter for anything that doesn’t fit the structured questions. Included in Share and Export. Tip: long-press any answer field to copy its contents to the clipboard.
renderpages_chapters_save_desc: Tap Save to save your answers. The app also auto-saves when you move away from an answer field.
renderpages_chapters_print_desc: Tap the print icon to print or save a chapter as PDF with your answers inline. Use Settings to print all chapters at once.
renderpages_chapters_share_desc: Tap Share at the bottom of any chapter to send your answers via WhatsApp, email, or any other app on your phone — useful for sharing with a group leader or mentor. See the for more.
renderpages_chapters_voice_desc: Tap the microphone icon inside any answer field to dictate your answer by voice — useful on mobile where typing can be slow. The button appears when your device supports speech input.
renderpages_chapters_answercheck_desc: Tap the check icon on eligible question cards for a quick offline check of your answer — it checks length, relevance, and whether your response engages with the passage. No internet needed. In Auto mode this runs automatically after each save; in Manual mode (the default) you tap it yourself. Change this in .
renderpages_chapters_readaloud_desc: A speaker icon on longer passages lets the app read them aloud. You can adjust which passages it appears on in <button class="howto-inline-link" onclick="renderSettings('app')">Settings</button> (using the Advanced interface mode of Settings).
renderpages_chapters_navbtns_desc: Back and Next buttons at the bottom of each chapter. You can also swipe left/right to move between chapters.
renderpages_chapters_other_desc: Some studies include additional features — such as short questionnaires or Go Deeper pop-ups with extra reading or background. These will make sense when you encounter them.
renderpages_library_loading_body: Tap the library icon in the top-left corner to open your Library. If you have received a .estudy file, tap Load Study at the bottom of the Library screen to import it. The study will then appear in your list and can be opened at any time.
renderpages_library_switching_body: All the studies you have loaded appear in the Library. Tap any one to open it. Your answers in each study are kept separately.
renderpages_library_removing_body: To remove a study from the Library, tap the 🗑️ icon on its card. This will delete the study and all your saved answers for it. Make sure you have shared or printed your answers first if you want to keep them.
renderpages_sharing_chapter_body: At the bottom of each chapter, tap Share to send your answers for that chapter via WhatsApp, email, or any other app on your phone. This is a great way to share your work with a group leader or mentor before a meeting.
renderpages_sharing_all_body: To share your answers from every chapter at once, go to Settings → Study → Data → Share all answers. This compiles everything into a single document.
renderpages_sharing_print_body: Tap the 🖸 icon on any chapter to print or save a PDF of that chapter with your answers filled in. To export all chapters as a single PDF, go to Settings → Study → Data → Print / export all chapters.
renderpages_sharing_format_body: In Settings you can choose between WhatsApp Formatted (bold text markers that WhatsApp renders as bold) and Plain Text (better for email). You can change this at any time.
renderpages_shareapp_know_body: If you think someone else would find this study helpful — a friend, a family member, someone in your church — you can share a link to the app using the button below.
renderpages_shareapp_download_intro: You can also download the app directly and install it on an Android device. The file is a password-protected zip — here’s how to get it working:
renderpages_shareapp_download_step1: Tap the button below to download the zip file from Google Drive.
renderpages_shareapp_download_note: (The reason for the password is that Google Drive flags .apk files; the password + zip bypasses this.)
renderpages_leaders_confidential: Intended for group leaders and mentors
renderpages_copyright_licence_link: To view a copy of this licence, visit creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0.
renderpages_copyright_share_entry: Share: copy and redistribute the material in any medium or format.
renderpages_copyright_adapt_entry: Adapt: remix, transform, and build upon the material for any purpose, even commercially.
renderpages_copyright_attribution_entry: Attribution: You must give appropriate credit, provide a link to the licence, and indicate if changes were made.
renderpages_copyright_sharealike_entry: ShareAlike: If you remix, transform, or build upon the material, you must distribute your contributions under the same licence as the original.
renderpages_settings_textsize_preview: The righteous will live by faith. What is born of the Spirit is spirit.
renderpages_settings_darkmode_desc: Easier on the eyes in low light
renderpages_settings_rememberplace_desc: On restart, return to the last chapter you were reading. Also show a Continue button on the title page.
renderpages_settings_readingfont_desc: Choose the font used for body text throughout the study
renderpages_settings_iconstyle_desc: Choose the style of icons used throughout the app. Takes effect when you next navigate.
renderpages_settings_answercheck_desc:
Manual — the answer-check icon appears on eligible question cards; tap it yourself when you want feedback.

Auto — feedback runs automatically after each answer is saved (when you move away from the field), but only if the answer has changed since the last check.
renderpages_settings_navbtns_desc: Show chapter navigation buttons at the bottom of each chapter
renderpages_settings_swipe_desc: How far you need to swipe to change chapter
renderpages_settings_answerbox_desc: How tall the answer field starts before you begin typing
renderpages_settings_myprogress_desc: A summary page showing how many questions you have answered in each chapter, with a completion ring.
renderpages_settings_notes_desc: A free-writing page after Ch. 9, e.g. for thoughts that don’t fit neatly into any specific chapter. Data is saved if page deactivated; it reappears when reactivated.
renderpages_settings_leaders_desc: Pastoral guidance for group leaders and mentors, with teaching points and notes on sensitivities for each chapter.
renderpages_settings_howto_desc: An introduction to the app — how to get the most from the study, how the questions work, and a guide to the app’s features.
renderpages_settings_about_desc: Background on the study — its aims, its approach, and the author.
renderpages_settings_autosave_desc: Show a brief confirmation when answers are auto-saved
renderpages_settings_shareformat_desc: WhatsApp Formatted adds bold markers for WhatsApp. Plain Text is better for email.
renderpages_settings_readaloud_desc: Tap the speaker icon on a passage or popup to have it read aloud. Choose when the icon appears.
renderpages_settings_answerbtn_desc: Show the AI tutor check button on question cards
renderpages_settings_appupdates_body:
This app is not yet available on Google Play (but it’s coming soon). In the meantime:

  • Google Drive: Updates can be downloaded directly from Google Drive.
  • Zipped: Because Google Drive is cautious about APK files, the file is wrapped in a password-protected zip file.
  • Password: The password is 1234.

To update:

  1. Tap the button below to download the zip.
  2. Unzip it using the password: 1234
  3. Open the APK to install.

(The reason for the password is that Google Drive flags .apk files; the password + zip bypasses this.)
renderpages_settings_interfacemode_choos: Choose how many features are visible. You can change this at any time.
renderpages_settings_mode_basic_desc: a clean reading experience with essential display settings only.
renderpages_settings_mode_intermediate_d: adds reading font, answer box size, optional pages, Library, and app updates.
renderpages_settings_wantmore_body: The app has an Interface Mode that controls how many options you are shown. Once you have got used to how the app works, try switching from Basic to Intermediate, or even Advanced. The Interface Mode switcher is at the bottom of this page.
renderpages_settings_shareall_desc: Compile all your saved answers across all nine chapters into a single document to share on WhatsApp, email, or any other app.
renderpages_settings_printall_desc: Compile your answers (as above) but formatted for printing or saving as a PDF file.
renderpages_settings_resetsettings_desc: Resets all your preferences (font size, dark mode, and other settings) back to their original defaults. The onboarding introduction will also play again. Your answers, notes, progress, and current reading position are not affected.
renderpages_settings_clearwarning_text: Clearing your answers is permanent and cannot be undone. All written answers across all nine chapters will be deleted from this device. There is no way to recover them.
renderpages_settings_clearwarning_sugges: You may want to use the Share or Print buttons above to save a copy of your answers before clearing them.
renderpages_settings_recent_desc:
Show studies you have opened recently.
  • Off — never shown.
  • >5 — appears once you have more than 5 studies.
  • On — always shown.
renderpages_settings_shelves_desc:
Show studies arranged by category.
  • Off — never shown.
  • >8 — appears once you have more than 8 studies.
  • On — always shown.
renderpages_settings_emptyshelves_desc:
Control whether empty categories are shown in the Shelves tab.
  • None — empty sections and shelves are hidden entirely.
  • Few — empty sections within populated shelves are shown in a muted style.
  • Many — empty shelves and empty sections are all shown.
  • All — empty shelves, empty sections, and empty subsections are all shown.
renderpages_settings_paths_desc:
Show learning pathways arranged by category.
  • Off — never shown.
  • >2 — appears once you have more than 2 studies.
  • On — always shown.
renderpages_settings_pathsamount_desc:
How many “pathways” (curated study sequences) should be shown.
  • One — just show the first pathway.
  • Few — top-level headings plus the first sub-heading in each.
  • Many — top-level headings plus the first two sub-headings.
  • All — all levels shown.
renderpages_settings_language_desc: Choose the language for the app interface. Your study content and answers are not affected.
progress_encourage_0: Ready to start – let's get going.
progress_encourage_24: You've made a good start. Keep going!
progress_encourage_39: You're making real progress. Well done.
progress_encourage_49: Getting close to halfway – how quickly can you get there?
progress_encourage_74: More than halfway through. Keep it up.
progress_encourage_89: You're in the home straight now.
progress_encourage_99: Almost there. You can do it!
progress_count_no_answers_start: No answers saved yet – start with Chapter 1
progress_count_pathway_all_done: All pathway questions answered – well done!
progress_detail_open_to_start: Open this study to begin tracking progress.
progress_detail_not_installed: This study hasn't been installed yet.
progress_notes_placeholder: Write anything here — there are no questions to answer and no right or wrong responses…
progress_notes_info_body:
This is an optional space for any notes or thoughts that don't fit neatly into a specific chapter — observations that span several chapters, questions you want to come back to, things you heard in a group discussion, or anything else you want to remember.

Use it however you find helpful, or ignore it entirely if you prefer. It won't affect your progress through the study. You can switch this page off again at any time in Settings.

If you switch it off, anything you've written here is kept safely and will reappear if you switch it back on.
search_empty_prompt:
Start typing to search questions,
passages, and your saved answers.
settings_clear_answers_body: This will permanently delete all your saved answers for this study. This cannot be undone.
settings_reset_body:
This will reset all your settings (font size, dark mode, and other preferences) back to their original defaults. The onboarding introduction will also play again on next launch.

Your answers, notes, progress, and current reading position will not be affected.
shareprint_no_answers_body: You haven't saved any answers yet. Work through some chapters first, then come back to export.
shareprint_print_fallback_chapter:
{studyTitle} — Chapter {number}: {chapterTitle}

(Print not available — use Share instead)
shareprint_print_fallback_all: Print not available on this device. Use Share all answers instead.
studyloader_delete_body: This permanently removes the study and all your saved answers. Have you shared or printed your work?
studyloader_version_older: This study file is older than expected. File: v{actual} · App expects: v{expected}. Please update the study file to the latest version.
studyloader_version_newer: This study file is newer than expected. File: v{actual} · App expects: v{expected}. Please update the app to the latest version.
studyloader_picker_body: Select a study data file (.estudy) to load its chapters, questions, and content.
studyloader_error_no_study_json: {filename} is a zip but contains no file ending in study.json.
utils_celebration_sub: Chapter {number} complete. Keep up the good work!
validation_tutor_input_placeholder: Ask a follow-up or send your answer…
validation_tutor_err_network: Couldn't reach the AI tutor — check your connection and try again.
validation_tutor_err_429: AI tutor is busy — please wait a moment and try again.
validation_tutor_err_auth: AI tutor: API key problem. Check study settings.
validation_tutor_err_404: AI tutor: model not found. Contact the study publisher.
validation_tutor_err_500: AI tutor: server error — try again in a moment.
validation_tutor_err_status: AI tutor: error {status} — please try again.
validation_tutor_err_unknown: AI tutor: something went wrong — please try again.
validation_lv_stuck_hint: It looks like you might be stuck. Here's a hint:
validation_lv_stuck_no_hint: It looks like you might be stuck. Try starting with what the passage makes you think, feel, or wonder about — even if you're not sure of the full answer.
validation_lv_too_short: That's a start — but can you say a little more? Even just a sentence or two will help you engage more deeply with the question.
validation_lv_repeats_question: It looks like your answer might be repeating the question. Try putting the idea into your own words.
validation_lv_repeats_passage: It looks like your answer might be repeating the passage. Try to answer the question from the passage, in your own words.
validation_lv_off_topic_hint: Your answer might be going in a different direction from what the question is asking (or maybe you just have a unique and interesting way of expressing yourself).
validation_lv_off_topic_no_hint: Your answer might be going in a different direction from what the question is asking (or maybe you just have a unique and interesting way of expressing yourself). Re-read the question carefully and double-check: What specific idea is it pointing you toward?
validation_lv_pass_thematic: Looks good. Your answer engages with the question — keep going.
validation_lv_pass_no_sample_no_hint: Your answer passes basic checks. This question doesn't have thematic data available, so no advanced check was done.
settings_resetall_body:
This will permanently erase everything: all downloaded studies, all answers and notes, all progress, all settings, and your reading position.

This cannot be undone.
settings_resetall_confirm2_body: Type reset to confirm. Every study, answer, and setting will be permanently deleted.
renderpages_settings_nuclear_warning_tex: The following action is irreversible. It will erase all downloaded studies, answers, notes, progress, and settings from this device.
renderpages_settings_nuclear_desc: Use this only if you want to start completely fresh, as if the app was just installed.
renderpages_newhere_upgradeslides_prompt: If you want to see the upgrade introduction slides again, tap this button:
